sections.paragraph:
μόλιβος, ου, ὁ (Hom.+; PTebt 121, 52; 84; LXX; TestAbr A 20 p. 103, 6f [μολύβδου; Stone p. 54]; GrBar 5:3 ‘leaden missile’; Jos., Bell. 6, 278, Ant. 15, 398. On the var. forms and spellings s. Thackeray 96; 116) lead, because of its low melting point a symbol of the earth, destroyed in the fire of the last judgment 2 Cl 16:3.—DELG s.v. μόλυβδος.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

var. var. = various(ly)

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 657
